topStories.description: Despite US-Israeli attacks aimed at crippling Iran's missile arsenal, the country continues missile launches daily. Estimates reveal only a third of missiles confirmed destroyed, while Iran asserts ongoing production and capability, posing potential risks to US interests.

topStories.title: Russian man, Barron Trump and ‘jealousy’: Matvei Rumiantsev assaulted woman on video call, get 4 years in jail
topStories.description: The case unexpectedly involved US President Donald Trump’s son Barron Trump, who told UK police he’d witnessed the attack during a video call last year.

topStories.title: Melania Trump calls for AI in education as US' student scores hit 20-year low: 35% 12th graders proficient in reading
topStories.description: Citing NAEP's 2024 national sampling, Melania Trump said that USA's average scores have reached their lowest levels in 20 years

topStories.title: US-based burger chain awarded  ₹15 crore in bonuses to employees under pressure
topStories.description: The demand was so high that several outlets suffered severe shortages and were even compelled to close early. Despite these challenges, employees managed long queues and handled the intense pressure well.

topStories.description: It can take up to 2 years to build a Tomahawk, costing $3.6 million a piece, according to the report. Moreover, last year's budget had included only 57 of them.

topStories.description: The meeting reviewed the 'close strategic cooperation' between Doha and Washington, Qatar's foreign ministry said.

dailyNews.title: Kattumannarkoil (SC): An agrarian constituency where even basic demands of farmers remain unfulfilled
dailyNews.description: Given the caste matrix of the constituency, where there are 2.24 lakh voters with a strong mix of Dalits and Vanniyars, it is the consolidation of these major groups that holds the key. dailyNews.description: Mexico's navy launches a rescue mission for ships delivering aid to Cuba, strained by food and fuel shortages amid a US embargo.

dailyNews.description: The US television presenter will return to hosting her NBC News programme in April.

dailyNews.title: Secret Service agent assigned to Jill Biden injured in 'negligent discharge' at Philadelphia airport
Meta vows to 'aggressively' fight after landmark verdicts find tech giant liable for addicting kids
dailyNews.description: Meta's chief legal officer said the company will aggressively appeal jury verdicts that found the company liable for designing addictive platforms for young users.

dailyNews.title: AOC says politicians, especially Democrats, should promise not to accept 'AI money'
dailyNews.description: Democratic Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez of New York said that politicians, particularly those of her own party, should vow not to accept "AI money."

dailyNews.title: WATCH: Airport travelers reveal what they truly think about ICE helping TSA with massive lines
dailyNews.description: Travelers at Houston’s George Bush Airport were very grateful for U.S. ICE agents lending TSA a helpful hand, as some said that they had been waiting in line for three to four hours.

dailyNews.title: 'Duck Dynasty' star Jase Robertson says 'rednecks' are the only people left who are 'unoffendable' in America
dailyNews.description: Jase Robertson says "rednecks" are the only people left who are "unoffendable," sharing the claim on his "Unashamed" podcast with Al Robertson and Zach Dasher.

dailyNews.title: Viral interview captures what traveler really thought about ICE agents helping TSA at airports
dailyNews.description: Brooklyn man Chris Scali went viral after praising ICE agents at Newark Airport, saying their presence was just "common sense" as the DHS shutdown played out.

dailyNews.title: Sheridan Gorman's university newspaper touts ICE tracker after freshman allegedly murdered by illegal alien
dailyNews.description: Loyola Phoenix still runs an ICE tracker after DHS says freshman Sheridan Gorman was allegedly killed by an illegal immigrant released under Biden.

topStories.description: A generation ago, foreign fury over the Iraq invasion often blurred into anti-Americanism. Now, some Europeans seem ready to distinguish between the president and the American people.

topStories.description: Thousands of demonstrations against the Trump administration are scheduled to take place in cities and towns across the country on Saturday.

topStories.title: Trump Delays Strikes on Iran's Energy Sector for 10 More Days
topStories.description: After nearly one month of war, the United States, Israel and Iran are continuing to trade air and drone strikes. Now President Donald Trump says they’re also trading potential peace plans but Iran is continuing to publicly dispute that any talks are underway. It comes as the president is extending a deadline he gave Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz of face attacks against its power plants. NBC’s Garrett Haake reports for TODAY from the White House.

dailyNews.title: Ambitious, authoritarian and talking to Trump? The hard-liners rising in Iran
dailyNews.description: The Israeli military has systematically killed Iran’s top leaders, so when President Donald Trump said this week that talks are being held with Iran, the biggest question was: Who exactly is the U.S. talking to

dailyNews.title: Covid and flu shots in limbo as Trump administration mulls appeal over vaccine panel
dailyNews.description: After a federal judge blocked Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s vaccine policies, the Justice Department has yet to appeal the decision — and ultimately may not.

dailyNews.title: Iran war rages, Strait of Hormuz still blocked as Trump pushes for a deal
dailyNews.description: The Trump administration expects Iran's formal response to its 15-point peace proposal today, as Tehran continues blocking the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: Iran maintains chokehold on Strait of Hormuz despite Trump's deadlines
dailyNews.description: The Iran war is nearing the one-month mark as different versions of the state of apparent negotiations between Tehran and the U.S. emerge. CBS News' Chris Livesay reports.

dailyNews.title: Senate approves most DHS funding, hours after Trump promised to restart TSA pay
dailyNews.description: The Senate agreed early Friday to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security in an effort to end a standoff in Congress that led to massive lines at many airports.

dailyNews.title: Iran live updates: More than 300 American troops wounded in Iran war
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.

dailyNews.title: WATCH:  Melania Trump showcases robot guest at WH summit
dailyNews.description: A walking, talking humanoid robot strolled side-by-side with first lady Melania Trump at the White House for the second day of her international technology summit.

dailyNews.title: Brent oil tops $110 again after Chinese ships are turned away from Strait of Hormuz
dailyNews.description: Oil prices traded higher after U.S. President Donald Trump said Iran had allowed 10 oil tankers to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

dailyNews.title: David Sacks says his time as Trump's crypto and AI czar has ended
dailyNews.description: The venture capitalist said he will still be a part of the White House's Technology committee and will help push Trump's AI plan forward.

dailyNews.title: Anthropic wins preliminary injunction in DOD fight as judge cites 'First Amendment retaliation'
dailyNews.description: A federal judge in San Francisco granted Anthropic's request for a preliminary injunction in its lawsuit against the Trump administration.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says Colombia Will Accept Deportees, Ending Tariff Standoff
dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.title: Trump Says He Wants to 'Clean Out' Gaza, Send Refugees to Egypt and Jordan
dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.
